page: Microsoft Azure E16-4ds_v4 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the EDSv4 family, virtualized via Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz, with 2 physical cores and 2 threads per core. The server is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per core. Additionally, it includes 614 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, while lacking any dedicated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate a clear performance profile characterized by moderate single-core capabilities but constrained multi-core and memory throughput. Single-core performance is average, as evidenced by a stress-ng single-core score of 1411.29 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1279.0. However, multi-core scaling is weak, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 3706.48 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 2827.0. Memory bandwidth is also relatively low, measuring 27.21 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 21.44 GB/sec for large-block write operations.

The primary tradeoff of the E16-4ds_v4 is its high memory density relative to its limited compute capacity. While the 128.0 GB of RAM and 614 GB local disk offer substantial capacity for memory-bound applications, the weak multi-core performance and low memory bandwidth limit its suitability for compute-intensive tasks, such as software compilation or ray tracing. This server is qualitatively cost-effective for workloads requiring large memory footprints and local storage but minimal parallel processing. It is best suited for memory-optimized applications, small-scale databases, and caching layers like Redis, provided the transaction volume does not saturate the CPU.
description: Microsoft Azure E16-4ds_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the EDSv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor at 2.6 GHz, 128.0 GB of RAM, and 614 GB of local storage. While single-core performance is average, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1279.0, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are weak, as shown by a stress-ng multi-core score of 3706.48 and large-block read bandwidth of 27.21 GB/sec. This hardware profile presents a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per core, making it qualitatively cost-effective for memory-heavy workloads that do not require intensive parallel computation. The server is best suited for memory-optimized databases, caching layers, and applications requiring substantial local storage capacity.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16-4ds_v4 is a memory-optimized server featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and 614 GB of local storage, designed for memory-intensive workloads with moderate compute needs.

tagline: A high-memory virtual server with local storage, offering a dense memory-to-vCPU ratio for memory-bound workloads with moderate processing requirements.

614 GB of local storage with premium storage capability
High memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per core
Average single-core performance but weak multi-core scaling
Best suited for memory-optimized databases and caching layers
